explanation: Pueraria lobata (kudzu) root extract is rich in puerarin, daidzin, and other isoflavones — phytoestrogen antioxidants with potent free-radical-scavenging, anti-inflammatory, and estrogen-receptor-mediated collagen-stimulating properties. It is well-tolerated and used in anti-aging and brightening formulas for its dense isoflavone antioxidant content.

Pulsatilla koreana (Korean pasqueflower) extract provides triterpenoid saponins and polyphenol antioxidants with anti-inflammatory and free-radical-scavenging properties, widely used in K-beauty formulations. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic extract concentrations and used in protective and calming formulas for its antioxidant content.

Pumice is a naturally occurring volcanic glass mineral used in cosmetics as a hard physical exfoliant and abrasive in scrubs and foot-care products for heavy callus removal. Its irregular particles make it too harsh for delicate facial skin; it is best suited to body and foot exfoliation on intact, non-sensitized skin.

Punica granatum (pomegranate) extract is rich in punicalagins, ellagic acid, and anthocyanins — among the most potent polyphenol antioxidants in the plant kingdom — with documented anti-inflammatory, brightening, and photoprotective properties. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and used in anti-aging and protective formulas for its extraordinary antioxidant density.

Punica granatum (pomegranate) seed extract provides punicic acid — a rare conjugated triene fatty acid — alongside tocopherols and polyphenol antioxidants with barrier-supportive and potent antioxidant properties. It is well-tolerated and used in anti-aging and nourishing formulas for its unique fatty acid profile and exceptional antioxidant content.

Purified water is deionized or distilled water used as the primary solvent and vehicle in water-based skincare formulas, in which all water-soluble ingredients are dissolved or suspended. It is, by definition, inert and universally well-tolerated.
This entry is a data artifact — a product claim or marketing descriptor rather than an INCI ingredient name. It does not represent a formulation ingredient or skin-active compound.

PVM/MA copolymer (polyvinyl methyl ether/maleic acid copolymer) is a synthetic vinyl polymer used as a film-former, thickener, and rheology modifier in skincare and haircare formulas. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations, functioning as a texture and stability modifier rather than a skin-active ingredient.

PVP (polyvinylpyrrolidone) is a synthetic water-soluble polymer widely used as a film-former, binder, and viscosity modifier in skincare, haircare, and cosmetic formulas, providing a flexible, substantive film on skin and hair. It is considered well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and non-comedogenic at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Pyrus malus (apple) fruit extract provides malic acid, pectin, polyphenols, and flavonoid antioxidants with mild AHA exfoliation, brightening, and free-radical-scavenging properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and antioxidant formulas for its combined mild chemical exfoliant and polyphenol content.

Pyrus malus fruit extract is apple fruit extract providing malic acid, pectin, and polyphenol antioxidants with mild AHA exfoliation and brightening properties — the same ingredient as Pyrus Malus (Apple) Fruit Extract, listed without the common name parenthetical. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and antioxidant formulas.

The apostrophe in "Pyrus'" is a data artifact from ingredient label parsing; this is the same ingredient as Pyrus Malus (Apple) Fruit Extract — apple fruit extract providing malic acid, polyphenols, and antioxidants with mild brightening and exfoliant properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening formulas.

The apostrophe in "Pyrus'" is a data artifact from ingredient label parsing; this is the same ingredient as Pyrus Malus Fruit Extract — apple fruit extract providing malic acid, polyphenols, and antioxidants. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and antioxidant formulas.

Quaternium-15 is one of the most potent formaldehyde-releasing preservatives used in cosmetics — it slowly hydrolyzes to release formaldehyde, which inhibits microbial growth but is itself a well-documented contact allergen, established sensitizer, and classified carcinogen at occupational exposure levels. It is one of the leading causes of preservative-related allergic contact dermatitis in skincare and has been progressively phased out in favor of safer preservative systems.

Raspberry isolates are standardized extracts or isolated active compounds from raspberry (Rubus idaeus) providing anthocyanins, ellagic acid, and quercetin antioxidants with potent free-radical-scavenging and anti-inflammatory properties. They are well-tolerated and used in protective and brightening formulas for their high antioxidant content.

Red 6 (D&C Red No. 6, lithol rubine BCA) is a synthetic azo dye used as a colorant in cosmetics and lip products, providing red-pink pigmentation. It is approved for cosmetic use by regulatory bodies; individuals with azo dye sensitivity should approach with caution.

Red 7 (D&C Red No. 7, lithol rubine BA) is a synthetic lake dye used as a colorant in lip products and color cosmetics, providing rich red pigmentation with good color stability. It is approved for cosmetic use; individuals with synthetic azo dye sensitivity should approach with caution.

This entry appears to be a label artifact combining a color shade name ("Red 7 Ruby:") with an ingredient — diisostearyl malate is a polyfunctional ester emollient used in lip products and color cosmetics for its non-sticky, cushioning skin feel and excellent pigment dispersion properties. Diisostearyl malate is well-tolerated and non-sensitizing.

Resveratrol is a stilbene polyphenol antioxidant naturally occurring in grape skins, Japanese knotweed, and certain berries, with potent free-radical-scavenging, anti-inflammatory, sirtuin-activating, and UV-protective properties documented across numerous in vitro and clinical studies. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and used in high-performance anti-aging and protective formulas for its exceptional antioxidant depth and documented skin-rejuvenating activity.
This entry is a data artifact — a category descriptor or marketing term rather than an INCI ingredient name. It does not represent a specific formulation ingredient or skin-active compound.

Retinol (vitamin A alcohol) is the most potent over-the-counter retinoid and the gold standard of evidence-backed anti-aging actives, converting in the skin to retinoic acid to stimulate collagen synthesis, accelerate cell turnover, and reduce fine lines, hyperpigmentation, and acne. It is flagged for barrier-disrupting potential — retinization (peeling, redness, photosensitivity) during the adjustment period is common — and requires a gradual low-concentration introduction, daily sun protection, and barrier-supporting co-ingredients to minimize irritation.

Retinyl palmitate is a retinol ester and the mildest form of vitamin A requiring two conversion steps to active retinoic acid, providing gentler retinoid activity with reduced irritation potential compared to retinol — though also with lower potency. It is flagged for barrier-disrupting potential at higher concentrations and shares retinol's photosensitivity and peeling risks; daily sun protection is essential during use.

Rh-oligopeptide-1 is recombinant human epidermal growth factor (rhEGF) — a protein signaling molecule that binds EGF receptors on keratinocytes and fibroblasts to stimulate cell proliferation, differentiation, collagen synthesis, and wound healing at concentrations as low as parts per billion. It is well-tolerated and particularly powerful in post-procedure, wound-healing, and anti-aging contexts for its receptor-level cell regeneration activity.

Riboflavin (vitamin B2) is used in skincare as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent with photoprotective co-factor properties and tissue-repair benefits linked to its essential role in cellular energy metabolism and redox cycling. It is biocompatible, non-irritating, and well-tolerated across all skin types; it imparts a characteristic yellow color to formulas at higher concentrations.

Rice ferment filtrate is the filtered liquid byproduct of rice (sake) fermentation, rich in kojic acid, ferulic acid, amino acids, and B vitamins with brightening, antioxidant, and skin-conditioning properties — historically associated with the luminous skin of Japanese sake brewery workers (toji). It is exceptionally well-tolerated and used in brightening and ferment-forward formulas for its combined brightening and nourishing properties.

Ricinus communis seed oil (castor oil) is uniquely rich in ricinoleic acid (~90%) — a hydroxylated fatty acid that provides exceptional occlusive moisture retention, a thick glossy texture, and strong film adhesion particularly suited for lip and hair applications. It is well-tolerated by most skin types; its thick viscosity and high ricinoleic acid content give it moderate-to-high comedogenic potential on acne-prone or congestion-prone skin.

Rosa canina fruit oil (rosehip seed oil) is one of the richest plant sources of linoleic acid (~44%) and alpha-linolenic acid (~33%), with additional carotenoids and traces of trans-retinoic acid providing potent barrier-repairing, brightening, and anti-aging properties. It is well-tolerated and particularly valued for dry, hyperpigmented, and mature skin; its high polyunsaturated fatty acid content makes it prone to oxidation, requiring antioxidant co-ingredients or opaque, sealed packaging.

Rosa canina fruit oil (rosehip seed oil) — the asterisk indicates organic certification — is one of the richest plant sources of linoleic and alpha-linolenic acid with potent barrier-repairing, brightening, and anti-aging properties. It is well-tolerated and particularly valued for dry, hyperpigmented, and mature skin; its high polyunsaturated content requires antioxidant protection and opaque packaging to prevent rancidity.

Rosa damascena (damask rose) flower extract provides polyphenols, flavonoids, and rose-derived aromatic compounds with antioxidant and skin-conditioning properties — however, damask rose and its derivatives contain geraniol, citronellol, eugenol, and linalool, all EU-regulated fragrance allergens. It carries sensitization risk on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin, particularly in leave-on products; the extract form carries lower risk than rose essential oil but is not fragrance-free.

Rosa gallica (Gallic rose) flower extract provides tannins, flavonoids, and polyphenol antioxidants with astringent and free-radical-scavenging properties. It is well-tolerated at cosmetic extract concentrations and used in protective and toning formulas for its antioxidant content; individuals with fragrance sensitivity should be aware that rose extracts may contain trace regulated allergens.
